title: WalletStore::new migration: user_version read before transactions; race on fresh DB
description: db.rs WalletStore::new() reads user_version once before all migration blocks: let version: i64 = sqlx::query_scalar(PRAGMA user_version).fetch_one(&pool). Each migration checks if version < N. If two processes open a brand-new DB simultaneously (version=0 in both reads) both will execute migration 1 through 8. CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS is idempotent so the table creation does not error, but PRAGMA user_version = N is not transactional across processes — both processes will commit their transactions and the final user_version will be whichever commits last. In practice with SQLite WAL and max_connections=1 per process this is unlikely to cause data corruption, but the version read outside any lock means two concurrent openers can both believe they are running migration N and both set PRAGMA user_version = N after completing it, which is correct only because the migrations are idempotent. If a future migration is not idempotent (e.g. a data migration rather than DDL) this pattern will cause silent double-execution. This is nie-br9a.12 class. Trigger: two processes race to open the same wallet DB for the first time. Bad behavior: future non-idempotent migration executed twice.
